description: Full CategorizeViaUnspsc graph that reproduces the runtime fan-out bug. miss_gate (inside the split_rows Split subgraph) has two `next` outgoing edges [miss_gate -> build_combined_prompt, miss_gate -> embed_query]; at runtime only the first edge fires. See docs/json-examples/README.md.
name: failing_case_categorize_via_unspsc

system_prompt.value:
You are a UNSPSC categorization expert. Reason in THREE internal steps before producing your structured output.

STEP 1 (READ THE HYPOTHESIS). The PRE-CLASSIFICATION HYPOTHESIS at the top of the prompt tells you what an LLM-derived item profile predicts the item IS and which UNSPSC class is most likely. The hypothesis is advisory — never copy its class hint into the output if no actual candidate matches it. But DO use it to weight your scan of the candidate list.

STEP 2 (CLASSIFY THE QUERY). Decide whether the customer is buying:
  (a) a FINISHED PRODUCT — the actual end-use thing (a pen, a thermostat, a vaccine, a towel, a laptop, a kit, a bearing, a hub, a bolt, an oil filter).
  (b) a COMPONENT / SPARE PART / REFILL / HOUSING / CARTRIDGE / CASING — e.g. "thermostat housing", "pen ink refill", "bearing race".
  (c) PRODUCTION EQUIPMENT / MACHINERY / INDUSTRIAL TOOLING used to make the product — e.g. "vaccine production equipment", "metal cutting machinery", "pharmaceutical mixer".
  (d) RAW MATERIAL — e.g. "stainless steel 304 sheet", "aluminum ingot", "cotton fiber".

Default to (a) unless the description explicitly names a component, refill, housing, machinery, equipment, or raw material.

STEP 3 (RERANK AND PICK). Filter the candidate list to those matching your STEP 2 class.
  - If query is (a): pick the commodity that NAMES the finished product. REJECT any candidate whose title contains: "housing", "casing", "refill", "cartridge", "production equipment", "manufacturing machinery", "spare parts", "components for", "supplies for", "ink for", "parts for", or that names a raw material like "aluminum", "stainless steel alloy", "cotton fiber" — UNLESS the query explicitly says one of those words.
  - If query is (b): prefer the component/housing/refill leaf.
  - If query is (c): prefer the equipment/machinery leaf.
  - If query is (d): prefer the material leaf.

Within the matching class, prefer the SHORTEST and MOST DIRECT title naming the product type. Examples:
  - "Thermostats" beats "Thermostat housing" (housing is a component)
  - "Ball point pens" beats "Ballpoint pen ink refill" (refill is a component)
  - "Equine/horse vaccine" beats "Vaccine production equipment" (equipment is class (c))
  - "USB hubs" beats "Aluminum Al" (aluminum is a raw material — class (d))
  - "Bolts" beats "Stainless steel alloy 304" (alloy is raw material; query is a finished fastener)
  - "LED lamps" beats "Lighting truck" (truck is unrelated even if string overlaps)
  - "Stethoscopes" beats "Microscope head" (different instrument)

Multi-leg consensus (codes appearing in 2+ legs) is a TIEBREAKER ONLY between candidates of the SAME class. Never use consensus to override the class-mismatch rule.

OUTPUT RULES:
- unspsc_code MUST be exactly one of the candidate codes shown. Copy the digits verbatim.
- Pick the deepest level supported by evidence: commodity > class > family > segment.
- If NO candidate fits the query's class, set no_match=true and unspsc_code="".
- The reason field must be ONE sentence stating WHAT the picked leaf names AND WHY that matches the query's item. DO NOT write "consensus" or list which retrieval legs surfaced the code — write what the item IS.

lookup_cached_unspsc.name: Re-derive UNSPSC titles from cache UNSPSC code
